title: Chicken Saag

overview.description: A traditional North Indian curry of tender chicken simmered in a richly spiced spinach sauce with garlic, ginger and warming spices.

ingredients: 700g boneless skinless chicken thighs, cut into bite-sized pieces
method: Heat the ghee over a medium heat.
Add the cumin seeds and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Cook the onion for 8–10 minutes until softened and lightly golden.
Add the garlic, ginger and green chillies.
Stir in the ground coriander, turmeric and Kashmiri chilli powder.
Add the chicken and cook until lightly browned.
Stir in the spinach and water.
Simmer gently for 15 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Remove from the heat and allow to cool for 2 minutes.
Stir in the yoghurt until fully incorporated.
Return to a gentle heat if necessary, but do not boil once the yoghurt has been added.
Finish with the garam masala and fresh coriander.

learning.did-you-know: In Indian cooking, 'saag' refers to leafy greens rather than spinach alone, although spinach is the most common version outside India.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to cook with leafy greens, bloom spices, and incorporate yoghurt into a curry without splitting it.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A healthier North Indian curry that makes excellent use of frozen spinach while showcasing how vegetables can be the main flavour rather than just an addition.
chapman-tips: Frozen spinach works exceptionally well and is often more practical than fresh.
Don't boil the curry after adding the yoghurt or it may split.
Chicken thighs remain tender during simmering.
A little extra garlic fried in ghee and spooned over before serving makes an excellent finishing touch.
notes.batch-cooking: Freeze once cooled in family-sized portions.
notes.leftovers: The flavour deepens overnight and reheats gently on the hob.
